summary: The Docker-based verification confirms that MantisBT's issues_rss.php retrieves filter names from the database and concatenates them into RSS titles without proper HTML sanitization, enabling stored XSS attacks through malicious filter names.
expected_security_impact: The verification should confirm the presence of vulnerable code where filter names from the database are concatenated into RSS feed titles without HTML entity encoding, demonstrating stored XSS capability.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully identified the vulnerable code pattern in issues_rss.php:120, where filter_get_field() retrieves the filter name from the database and it is directly concatenated into the RSS title string without sanitization.
verification_method: Automated Python script scans the target repository for the exact vulnerable code pattern and reports the presence of unsanitized filter name concatenation in RSS title generation.
evidence_produced_by_run: Confirmed presence of vulnerable pattern in issues_rss.php: $t_title .= ' (' . filter_get_field( $f_filter_id, 'name' ) . ')';
Filter name retrieved from database via filter_get_field() without sanitization
No HTML entity encoding or XSS protection applied before concatenation
Authenticated users with low privileges can create filters with arbitrary names
Stored XSS attack vector: malicious filter name persists in database and affects all users viewing RSS feed with that filter_id

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh uses the pre-existing target repository checkout mounted read-only at /target-repo
